source.definition: to acquire knowledge or skills through study, experience, or teaching

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "learn" originates from Old English "leornian", meaning to get knowledge or experience.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „learn” provine din engleza veche „leornian”, care înseamnă a dobândi cunoștințe sau experiență.

source.additional: Regular verb, commonly used in educational contexts.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a învăța“ comes from the Late Latin „invītiare“ (to instruct, to teach), which evolved to mean learning.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a învăța“ provine din latinul târziu „invītiare” (a informa, a instrui), care a evoluat cu sensul de „a învăța”.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Frequently used in academic contexts.

explanation.sourceText: This means to repeat something from memory.
explanation.targetText: Înseamnă a repeta ceva din memorie.

examples.sourceText: I have to memorize the vocabulary.
examples.targetText: Trebuie să învăț vocabulele pe de rost.

explanation.sourceText: This means to learn by one's own experiences.
explanation.targetText: Înseamnă a învăța prin experiență proprie.

examples.sourceText: I learn a lot through practical experience.
I do self-study in various subjects.
examples.targetText: Fac studiu individual la diferite materii.

examples.sourceText: I learn new things every day.
examples.targetText: Eu învăț lucruri noi în fiecare zi.

examples.sourceText: Are you studying for the exam?
She learns the new language quickly.
We study together for the exam.
Are you learning the vocabulary?
They learn diligently.
I have learned German.
They have learned many things.
He always studied diligently at school.
We had already learned a lot before the course started.
examples.targetText: Noi învățasem deja multe înainte de a începe cursul.

examples.sourceText: I will learn a new topic now.
examples.targetText: Voi învăța acum un subiect nou.

examples.sourceText: She will learn a lot after class.
examples.targetText: Ea o să învețe mult după cursuri.

examples.sourceText: I would learn more if I had more time.
examples.targetText: Aș învăța mai mult dacă aș avea mai mult timp.

examples.sourceText: It is important that you learn every day.
examples.targetText: Este important să înveți în fiecare zi.

examples.sourceText: Learn the new words!
Learn the vocabulary!
The learned knowledge is valuable.
The course is now learned.
By learning about the topic, I understand it better.
examples.targetText: Învățând despre acest subiect, înțeleg mai bine.

learningTips.text: Remember that "learn" and Romanian "a învăța" both mean to acquire knowledge or skills, whether at school or in everyday life.
The Romanian verb endings in present tense are: -u (I), -i (you singular), -ă (he/she), -ăm (we), -ați (you plural), -ă (they). Try to associate these with English pronouns.
Use the phrase "Învățăm împreună" (We learn together) to practice conjugation and remember the communal aspect of learning.
Think of the English word "invest" to remember the Latin root "invītiare" related to teaching and learning—investing time in knowledge.
